summary: McKenzie describes his experiment with image ad blending on BuySellAds, where he designed a banner ad for Bingo Card Creator that visually matches the host site's content style. The technique exploits banner blindness by making ads look like native content rather than traditional advertisements. He placed a $135/month ad on BusyTeacher.org targeting teachers and achieved a 2.2% click-through rate, far above banner advertising norms. The cost per acquisition dropped to roughly 8 cents versus 30-40 cents on Google AdWords, making the economics dramatically better. He reflects on the ethics of the approach and concludes it's aggressive but honest. He wishes more inventory were available in his niche and plans to pursue direct ad buys with smaller sites.
key_points: Ad blending — styling ads to resemble site content — defeats banner blindness and dramatically increases click-through rates
A $135/month BuySellAds placement on BusyTeacher.org yielded a 2.2% CTR and ~8 cent CPA versus 30-40 cents on Google AdWords
The ad is honest about being an ad but designed to not trigger automatic visual filtering by users
Direct ad buys can outperform Google AdWords on both cost and targeting, but finding appropriately-sized inventory is the bottleneck
The economics work out to roughly $4 to generate a $30 sale instead of $20 through AdWords
money_quotes: You want to not look like an ad at the first glance, but to look like an ad on the second glance.
Once upon a time I was an engineer totally scornful of effective marketing, but I have gradually gotten over it.
A lot of users think that yellow is used for marking the best results. Non-technical customers, yay.
That ad has a 2.2% click through rate, astronomical by the standards of banner advertising.
At typical conversion rates to a trial of about 1.8~2%, that means that this costs me $4 or so to generate a $30 sale rather than $20. Sold!
For an experiment that looks like it will net in the neighborhood of $1,000 a month in sales for $135 and ~10 minutes of pixel pushing, this one is going in the win column.
key_insight: Designing image ads that blend with a site's visual style defeats banner blindness and can dramatically reduce customer acquisition costs compared to standard ad networks.
